description: Tasks rewritten to match actual generated data

tasks.instruction: Find a Python quickstart example for running a text classification batch evaluation in the docs and modify its configuration to use a lightweight "mini" model variant, exactly two classification metrics (one measuring overall correctness and one combining precision and recall), and a maximum of 200 examples.
tasks.name: Find a Python quickstart example for running a text classification batch evaluation in the docs and modify its configuration to use a lightweight "mini" model variant, exactly two classification metrics (one measuring overall correctness and one combining precision and recall), and a maximum of 200 examples.
tasks.description: Find a Python quickstart example for running a text classification batch evaluation in the docs and modify its configuration to use a lightweight "mini" model variant, exactly two classification metrics (one measuring overall correctness and one combining precision and recall), and a maximum of 200 examples.

tasks.instruction: In the REST API docs, locate the batch evaluation creation endpoint’s curl example and update its URL to use a concrete project identifier and pagination query parameters with a limit of 50 and a non-empty cursor value.

target_names: API example: Create batch evaluation run (v1) curl request

tasks.name: In the REST API docs, locate the batch evaluation creation endpoint’s curl example and update its URL to use a concrete project identifier and pagination query parameters with a limit of 50 and a non-empty cursor value.
tasks.description: In the REST API docs, locate the batch evaluation creation endpoint’s curl example and update its URL to use a concrete project identifier and pagination query parameters with a limit of 50 and a non-empty cursor value.

tasks.instruction: From the metrics documentation, open the detailed page for the metric related to token-based billing and adjust its JSON configuration example so the per-1k-token price is increased from the default while leaving the currency and unit fields unchanged.
tasks.name: From the metrics documentation, open the detailed page for the metric related to token-based billing and adjust its JSON configuration example so the per-1k-token price is increased from the default while leaving the currency and unit fields unchanged.
tasks.description: From the metrics documentation, open the detailed page for the metric related to token-based billing and adjust its JSON configuration example so the per-1k-token price is increased from the default while leaving the currency and unit fields unchanged.

tasks.instruction: Using the configuration builder guide, set up a text generation comparison evaluation with two candidate models and one baseline, assigning each variant a distinct identifier, giving the candidates different nonzero temperatures and the baseline a zero temperature, limiting the run to 50 samples, and enabling only a correctness metric and a response-time metric before generating the config.

target_names: Comparison evaluation with config builder example

tasks.name: Using the configuration builder guide, set up a text generation comparison evaluation with two candidate models and one baseline, assigning each variant a distinct identifier, giving the candidates different nonzero temperatures and the baseline a zero temperature, limiting the run to 50 samples, and enabling only a correctness metric and a response-time metric before generating the config.

tasks.instruction: Via the docs search, open the JavaScript SDK page that documents the helper for running batch evaluations, then edit its example call so the options specify a concurrency level of 10 and copy the updated JavaScript snippet.
tasks.name: Via the docs search, open the JavaScript SDK page that documents the helper for running batch evaluations, then edit its example call so the options specify a concurrency level of 10 and copy the updated JavaScript snippet.
tasks.description: Via the docs search, open the JavaScript SDK page that documents the helper for running batch evaluations, then edit its example call so the options specify a concurrency level of 10 and copy the updated JavaScript snippet.

tasks.instruction: In the examples gallery, filter for evaluations that use the BLEU metric on datasets with at least 1000 samples, open the third matching example, and capture the REST API endpoint path shown for it.

target_names: EN–DE translation v2 benchmark with BLEU example

tasks.name: In the examples gallery, filter for evaluations that use the BLEU metric on datasets with at least 1000 samples, open the third matching example, and capture the REST API endpoint path shown for it.

tasks.instruction: Switch the documentation to use the newer API version instead of the default, then in the API reference open the dataset creation endpoint and copy the curl example that targets that newer version’s dataset creation path.

target_names: API example: Create dataset (v2) curl request

tasks.instruction: In the interactive playground, configure a text classification evaluation that emphasizes accuracy more than latency (for example a roughly 70/30 weight split), uses a sample size of 10, gives the run a short test name, and then start the evaluation.

target_names: Playground weighted accuracy and latency demo
